tactical_shape: Midfield rotations and a floating pentagon of play, with Weston McKennie, Tyler Adams and Malik Tillman forming the key starting trio
Likely to try to use possession to pull Australia’s back two lines out of shape and create gaps
standout_players: Weston McKennie, Tyler Adams and Malik Tillman were described by Gustavo Alfaro as ‘floating’ and a key part of a ‘pentagon’ of play
home_read.momentum: Coming off an emphatic 4-1 opener, but the punditry urges restraint and expects a very different, tougher test against Australia.

tactical_shape: Organised defence, intensity and a physical battle first approach
Happy to give opponents possession and look to hurt them in transition/direct play
Can play for the draw while remaining dangerous on the break
standout_players: Nestory Irankunda was singled out as a superpower and a player of the match against Turkey, with speed, determination without the ball and direct running highlighted
Mo Touré was described as a vital outlet when Australia are under pressure with the ball
fatigue_injuries: Aiden O’Neill was only able to walk the day after the Turkey game
Mo Touré has a calf concern
away_read.momentum: They impressed by stunning Turkey and now have a pragmatic route to the knockout rounds, with rotation and discipline likely central to their progress.
